mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the signal predictive power vanishes in high-inflation sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and persistent consumer price inflation forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive when both thresholds are breached, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which reduces construction sector earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in unemployment confirm a synchronized supply-side recovery, which improves corporate output efficiency and drives positive equity returns during post-recession phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances during recovery periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample tests.
